title: Garage & DIY Video Capture — LATAM
description:
As a Garage & DIY Capture contributor, you record first-person video of vehicle maintenance, tool use, hobbyist projects, and workshop tasks. The data trains AI systems for power-tool safety, vehicle diagnostics, and home-improvement assistants.

Scenes include oil changes, basic auto work, woodworking, electrical projects, and tool storage organization. A typical session is one project, 30–90 minutes long.

Strong fit for hobbyists, gearheads, and anyone with regular workshop time.

This variant is open to contributors across Latin America — Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Colombia, Chile, and Peru. Pay is $7/hour USD, fixed flat rate, paid via international transfer (Wise, Payoneer, or local equivalent) on a bi-weekly cadence. USD purchasing power makes this a meaningful supplemental income across the region.

Claru contractors are independent — no fixed hours, no minimum commitment. You take captures that fit your schedule and skip the ones that don't.
skills: Access to a garage, workshop, or DIY space
Familiarity with hand and power tools
Ability to follow scene briefs precisely
locationRequirements: MX, BR, AR, CO, CL, PE

faqs.question: Do I need expensive tools?
faqs.answer: No — we need real-world tool use, not professional setups. A basic socket set, drill, and saw covers most briefs.

faqs.question: Can I record dangerous tasks like grinding or welding?
faqs.answer: Only if you have the safety equipment you'd normally use. PPE in frame is a feature, not a bug — we want realistic safety practices captured.

faqs.question: How much does Garage & DIY capture pay in LATAM?
faqs.answer: LATAM contributors (MX, BR, AR, CO, CL, PE) are paid $7/hour USD flat, via Wise/Payoneer, bi-weekly.

faqs.question: When do I get paid?
faqs.answer: Bi-weekly. The first batch typically lands 7–10 days after your first approved capture.
